zh.lifeSummary: Jane Arblasted是Sir Thomas De La Roche, Baron Roche of Fermoy and Roch, Sheriff of Cork, MP的女兒，於1272年出生在威爾士卡馬特史爾。她於1304年與William Reade在威爾士卡馬特史爾卡馬特結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。她去世於1360年在威爾士享年88歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Jane Arblasted是Sir Thomas De La Roche, Baron Roche of Fermoy and Roch, Sheriff of Cork, MP的女儿，于1272年出生在威尔士卡馬特史爾。她于1304年与William Reade在威尔士卡馬特史爾卡馬特结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。她去世于1360年在威尔士享年88岁。

nameMeanings.name: Arblasted
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): occupational name from Anglo-Norman French alblaster, arblaster, Old French arbalestier, arbelestier ‘soldier armed with a crossbow’. The surname may also mean ‘maker of crossbows’.

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. en.lifeSummary: Jane Arblasted was born in 1272, in Carmarthenshire, Wales as the daughter of Sir Thomas De La Roche, Baron Roche of Fermoy and Roch, Sheriff of Cork, MP. She married William Reade in 1304, in Carmarthen, Carmarthenshire, Wales. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. She died in 1360, in Wales, at the age of 88.

fr.lifeSummary: Jane Arblasted est née en 1272, à Carmarthenshire, Galles, fille de Sir Thomas De La Roche, Baron Roche of Fermoy and Roch, Sheriff of Cork, MP. Elle a épousé William Reade en 1304, à Carmarthen, Carmarthenshire, Galles. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Elle est décédée en 1360, à Galles, à l’âge de 88 ans.
